title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 15)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's 74 Crabtree Lane, a 4,232 sq ft colonial house built in 2004, sold for $1,380,000.
Amesbury's 473 Main St, a colonial house from 1850, sold for $2,450,000, the highest in its area.
Ashland's 25 Hardwick Road, a 2,954 sq ft colonial house on a 47,424 sq ft lot, sold for $1,475,000.

title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett firefighter John Benoit, 63, died from occupational cancer after over 30 years of service.
Benoit followed a family legacy, joining the Everett Fire Department after serving in the Air Force.
His family requested donations to the 100 Club of Massachusetts instead of flowers for the funeral.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz were arrested in Dorchester for allegedly running a multi-state human trafficking operation.
The victim was coerced into sex work while homeless, moved between locations, and held against her will, prosecutors say.
Recci and Ortiz face charges including kidnapping, human trafficking, and various weapon and drug offenses, with a hearing set for July 16.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, 17, drowned at South Watuppa Pond in Fall River on July 12, authorities confirmed.
His mother, Kailyn Lord, described him as a 'brilliant, unique soul' with plans for a therapeutic horse ranch.
Police recovered his body after a two-hour search; foul play is not suspected, ongoing investigation continues.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke performance in Nantucket with co-stars from 'The Five-Star Weekend'.
The karaoke night took place on September 30, 2025, at Gaslight, featuring Garner singing 'Dancing Queen' with local guide Bill Jamieson.
Garner hailed Jamieson as a 'legend' and highlighted Nantucket's attractions, mentioning her visits to local museums and shops.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Smoke from Canadian wildfires has caused yellow skies in Boston, reducing expected heat levels, per the National Weather Service.
Meteorologists expect another wave of smoke to impact the region, bringing hazy skies and a potential smokey smell.
Forecasters predict the wildfire smoke will linger, possibly affecting weather conditions into Thursday.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A special traffic cone signed by Glasgow Chamber of Commerce's Stuart Patrick was sent to Boston via Delta Air Lines.
The cone symbolizes a new partnership and sister city relationship between Boston and Glasgow, aiming to strengthen cross-Atlantic ties.
Proceeds from the cone's tour in Boston will benefit mental health charities in Massachusetts and Scotland.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three more MBTA employees arrested for overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspections at Cabot Yard.
Defendants allegedly spent on-duty hours on personal activities, including fixing private vehicles and falsifying reports.
Seven former MBTA employees, including a supervisor, face federal charges and have pleaded not guilty in court.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won the last quarterfinal in Foxborough, ending Boston's World Cup hosting duties.
Hosting the World Cup cost Massachusetts taxpayers nearly $100 million, primarily for transportation improvements.
Boston's prior Olympic bid estimated $4.7 billion in costs, far exceeding World Cup expenditures.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A North Carolina judge issued an arrest order for Jen Belichick after a missed court appearance over a speeding citation.
Jen Belichick, married to Steve Belichick, was cited for driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County.
Bill Belichick is UNC's head coach since 2024, with a 5-year, $50 million contract; Steve Belichick is the team's defensive coordinator.
